relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: YHWH speaks into the freedom morning.
Every first opening of life belongs to the Liberator.
Remember the day of leaving the house of slavery.
The people go out in Aviv, the season of newness.
The liberation service will be kept in milk-and-honey promise.
Seven days of unleavened bread become festival.
Leaven disappears from the borders of memory.
The child is told: this is because of what YHWH did for me.
The sign rests on hand, between eyes, and in the mouth.
The appointed time is kept from days to days.
Promise enters the land named by oath.
The first opening of womb is passed over to YHWH.
Human firstborn are redeemed.
When the child asks, liberation is told again.
Pharaoh's hardness becomes the reason memory must redeem life.
The strong hand becomes sign on hand and brow.
Divine does not lead the traumatized people by the nearest war-road.
They turn by wilderness toward Sea of Reeds.
Joseph's bones travel with the promise.
They camp at the edge of wilderness.
YHWH goes before them as cloud by day and fire by night.
Guidance does not turn aside from the people.

contemplative_note: Exodus 13 is embodied memory: freedom goes on hand, brow, mouth, calendar, children, firstborn redemption, and the guiding cloud-fire that refuses to abandon the newly freed.
